story_constraints.vocabulary: Kindergarten (200-300 sight words), simple single-clause sentences, bright emotional language.
story_constraints.tone: Warm, safe, caring. Short wonder-packed beats. Parent reads aloud; child feels seen and kind.

poetic_structure.description: Prose follows a 6/4/6/4 syllable cadence with a gentle Resolution in Acts 4-5.

acts.title: The Wish & The Restlessness

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] notices someone in the [WORLD_NAME] who seems sad or left out. The world feels a little less bright.
objective.emotional: Child sees themselves in the character. The wish to help is validated.
objective.educational: First gentle seed: noticing others' feelings is kind.

key_dialogue_lines: Someone looks sad.
I want to help but I don't know how.
The [WORLD_NAME] feels quieter when someone is alone.
humor_or_magic_moments: Character tries to wave and almost trips.
A little creature nearby mirrors the character's worried face.
Stars or flowers seem to lean toward the one who is sad.

acts.title: The World & The Ally

objective.narrative: [ALLY_NAME] appears. Ally is kind, warm, and shows [CHARACTER_NAME] that kindness starts with noticing.
objective.emotional: A friend has arrived. Character learns they don't have to help alone.
objective.educational: Ally models empathy through gentle words and presence.

key_dialogue_lines: Hello! I am [ALLY_NAME], said the kind [ALLY_TYPE].
When someone is sad, the kindest thing is to be near.
You already have a kind heart. Let's use it together.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally does something sweet (shares a flower, or nods with big eyes).
Ally movement is soft (gentle steps, warm glow).
Ally and [CHARACTER_NAME] walk slowly toward the one who is sad.
poetic_sample_template: With soft kind eyes and care.

acts.title: The Challenge & The Ability

objective.narrative: A small creature or friend needs comfort. [CHARACTER_NAME] uses [SPECIAL_ABILITY] and kindness to help.
objective.emotional: Character feels that their kindness matters. Helping feels good.
objective.educational: Kindness is an action: sitting with, sharing, or using their special power gently.

key_dialogue_lines: Use your special power with kindness!
[CHARACTER_NAME] sat beside them. And it helped!
Being kind means being there.
humor_or_magic_moments: [SPECIAL_ABILITY] creates a gentle effect (soft glow, warm sparkles).
Ally nods with joy. The sad one gives a small smile.
The world feels a little brighter as [CHARACTER_NAME] is kind.

acts.title: The Transformation

objective.narrative: The one who was sad feels better. [CHARACTER_NAME] feels warm and proud. The world is peaceful.
objective.emotional: Earned moment. Kindness made a difference.
objective.educational: One gentle line: kindness helps everyone feel safe and loved.

key_dialogue_lines: I did it. I was kind.
When you are kind, you make the world a little better.
Kindness is like a warm blanket for the heart.
humor_or_magic_moments: The once-sad creature now plays or rests peacefully.
Ally curls up beside [CHARACTER_NAME] and the new friend.
A shared moment of quiet happiness.

acts.title: The Resolution & Rest

objective.narrative: All settle together. [CHARACTER_NAME] and [ALLY_NAME] and the new friend find a soft resting place. Peace and sleep.
objective.emotional: Child is mirrored into rest. Kindness leads to peace.
objective.educational: Kindness as superpower is felt; closing rhythm is soft and reassuring.

key_dialogue_lines: Rest now, kind [CHARACTER_NAME].
Sweet dreams. Tomorrow you will wake ready to be kind again.
Kindness is a superpower. It makes you and others feel good.
humor_or_magic_moments: A rhyming couplet closes the page with the slowest line.
Ally watches over [CHARACTER_NAME] in complete safety.
Read this page barely above a whisper.

instructions: Generate pages 4-13 only using the 5-act architecture above.
Maintain 5-8 words per sentence and 1-2 short sentences per paragraph.
Keep total output between 100-150 words.
Use warm, safe, caring language.
Use poetic rhythm with a gentle 6/4/6/4 feel when possible.
Apply gentle Resolution in Acts 4-5 (softer, peaceful wording).
Do not lecture; teach through imagery and action.
Ensure [LEARNING_THEME] shapes challenge and insight. End with calm, restful close.

story_constraints.vocabulary: Grade 2-4 chapter-book range, compound sentences, and gently introduced metaphor.
story_constraints.tone: Adventurous and warm. Challenge feels real but emotionally safe. Child reads along with parent.

poetic_structure.description: Prose rhythm with consistent paragraph length. Pages 12-13 close with a lullaby-style cadence.

acts.title: The Wish & The Restlessness

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] notices someone in the [WORLD_NAME] who is left out or hurting. They feel a pull to help but are unsure.
objective.emotional: The child recognizes the wish to be kind and the fear of not knowing how.
objective.educational: Noticing others' feelings is the first step in kindness.

key_dialogue_lines: I see someone who looks sad. I want to help.
What if I say the wrong thing?
Maybe being there is enough.
humor_or_magic_moments: A confident step is interrupted by a stumble.
The world reflects the moment (leaves still, light soft).
Metaphor: kindness waiting like a seed to grow.
prose_sample_template: In the [WORLD_NAME], [CHARACTER_NAME] saw someone sitting alone, and something in their chest said: go.
[CHARACTER_NAME] did not know what to do yet - only that they wanted to help.

acts.title: The World & The Ally

objective.narrative: [ALLY_NAME], a warm and observant [ALLY_TYPE], appears and invites [CHARACTER_NAME] to practice kindness together.
objective.emotional: Connection reduces fear. Kindness becomes a shared journey.
objective.educational: Ally models empathy and gentle action.

key_dialogue_lines: You do not have to fix everything. Being there is a kind act.
Come with me. We will show them they are not alone.
Kindness starts with one step.
humor_or_magic_moments: Ally has a charming mishap before recovering with warmth.
World responds to their presence (light softens, sounds gentle).
Character and ally share a small moment of courage.
prose_sample_template: [ALLY_NAME] came beside [CHARACTER_NAME] with a calm that felt like a lantern.
Together they moved toward the one who was sad, and the [WORLD_NAME] seemed to make space for them.

acts.title: The Challenge & The Ability

objective.narrative: A meaningful moment: the one who is sad needs comfort. [CHARACTER_NAME] uses [SPECIAL_ABILITY] and presence with [ALLY_NAME]'s support.
objective.emotional: Courage is reframed as kindness in action, tied to [LEARNING_THEME].
objective.educational: Kindness is a choice we make even when we are unsure.

key_dialogue_lines: Kindness is not about being perfect. It is about showing up.
Your [SPECIAL_ABILITY] can help - use it gently.
You are enough, [CHARACTER_NAME].
humor_or_magic_moments: [SPECIAL_ABILITY] creates a soothing effect that opens connection.
Ally balances seriousness with one warm line.
The world visibly softens as [CHARACTER_NAME] acts with kindness.
prose_sample_template: [CHARACTER_NAME] took a breath and stepped closer. They did not have all the words - but they had presence.
[ALLY_NAME] stayed beside them. Together they offered what they could: attention, warmth, and one small act of [SPECIAL_ABILITY].

acts.title: The Transformation

objective.narrative: The one who was sad begins to feel seen. [CHARACTER_NAME] feels the reward of kindness: connection and peace.
objective.emotional: Character can name what they learned through [LEARNING_THEME] and feels earned pride.
objective.educational: One gentle line: kindness strengthens relationships and helps everyone rest easier.

key_dialogue_lines: I was scared, and I still showed up.
That is [LEARNING_THEME], and you practiced it tonight.
When we are kind, we all feel a little safer.
humor_or_magic_moments: A small smile from the one who was sad.
World details transition from tension to resting warmth.
Ally mirrors calm beside [CHARACTER_NAME].
prose_sample_template: As the moment passed, [CHARACTER_NAME] felt a different kind of strength: quiet, real, and shared.
"Kindness is a gift you give and receive," [ALLY_NAME] said softly.

acts.title: The Resolution & Rest

objective.narrative: [CHARACTER_NAME] and [ALLY_NAME] and the new friend reach a peaceful moment. The [WORLD_NAME] settles into a soft, restful close.
objective.emotional: Peace is transferred from character to reader through slower cadence.
objective.educational: Kindness as strength is reinforced; closing ritual is soft and reassuring.

key_dialogue_lines: Rest now, kind [CHARACTER_NAME]; you did something important tonight.
Tomorrow you will wake ready to be kind again.
Breathe in calm, breathe out care; the night holds you.
humor_or_magic_moments: Final image emphasizes safety, warmth, and belonging.
A two-line lullaby close ends the story at its slowest pace.
Voice target is soft and steady.
lullaby_close_template: The [WORLD_NAME] is quiet, and kindness keeps watch.
[ALLY_NAME] stays near while your breathing grows slow.
Rest now, kind [CHARACTER_NAME], the night is on your side.
Sleep now, dear heart, and gently let go.

Residual worry gives way to grounded warmth.

objective.narrative: The world settles into deep safety. [CHARACTER_NAME] chooses rest with intention.
objective.emotional: The ending mirrors the reader's own breathing and closes with belonging and release.
